questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'Despite the ____ conditions underground, the crew completed the survey without incident.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Despite' indicates that the work succeeded in spite of difficulty.

**Working.** Difficulty implies adverse conditions — 'hazardous'; the other words describe easy conditions.

**Answer.** hazardous.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The length of the diagonal of a rectangle of sides $8$ and $6$ is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The diagonal is the hypotenuse of a right triangle with the two sides as legs.

**Working.** $\sqrt{8^2+6^2}=\sqrt{100}=10$.

**Answer.** $10$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: 'After the long shift, the foreman decided to call it a day.' This means he decided to:
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Call it a day' is an idiom meaning to stop an activity for the time being.

**Working.** In context, the foreman chose to end work for the day.

**Answer.** stop work for the day.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A triangle is divided into four smaller triangles by joining the midpoints of its sides (shown). The total number of triangles is _____ .

figure.caption: A triangle with its three midpoints joined, forming four small triangles.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><polygon points='80,15 20,120 140,120' fill='none' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='50' y1='67' x2='110' y2='67' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='50' y1='67' x2='80' y2='120' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='110' y1='67' x2='80' y2='120' stroke='#6366f1' stroke-width='2'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Count the small triangles and the original.

**Working.** Joining midpoints makes $4$ small triangles; with the original large triangle the total is $4+1=5$.

**Answer.** $5$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Monthly rainfall (in mm) over four months is $10, 20, 30, 40$ (see chart). The total rainfall over the four months is _____ mm.

figure.caption: Bar chart of monthly rainfall: 10, 20, 30, 40 mm.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><line x1='30' y1='160' x2='220' y2='160' stroke='#94a3b8' stroke-width='1'/><rect x='45' y='135' width='28' height='25' fill='#0ea5e9'/><rect x='90' y='110' width='28' height='50' fill='#0ea5e9'/><rect x='135' y='85' width='28' height='75' fill='#0ea5e9'/><rect x='180' y='60' width='28' height='100' fill='#0ea5e9'/><text x='52' y='173' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>M1</text><text x='187' y='173' font-size='9' fill='#94a3b8'>M4</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Total = sum of monthly values.

**Working.** $10+20+30+40 = 100$ mm.

**Answer.** $100$ mm.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: If $a:b = 2:3$ and $b:c = 4:5$, then $a:c$ equals
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Make $b$ common by scaling the two ratios.

**Working.** Scale to $a:b:c = 8:12:15$, hence $a:c = 8:15$.

**Answer.** $8:15$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Find the next term: $1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, \,?$
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Fibonacci: each term is the sum of the two preceding terms.

**Working.** $5+8 = 13$.

**Answer.** $13$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Find the odd one out: Coal, Petroleum, Natural gas, Granite.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Group by a common property and spot the exception.

**Working.** Coal, petroleum and natural gas are fossil fuels; granite is an igneous rock, not a fuel.

**Answer.** Granite.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'The auditor's ____ examination revealed discrepancies that others had overlooked.'
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Detecting overlooked discrepancies requires a very thorough, careful examination.

**Working.** 'Scrupulous' means diligent and exact; 'cursory' (hasty) would miss details.

**Answer.** scrupulous.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: In a square split into four quadrants, the shaded quadrant moves clockwise each step: top-left, top-right, bottom-right, ... The next shaded quadrant is

figure.caption: Three squares with the shaded quadrant at top-left, top-right and bottom-right respectively.
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' role='img'><g stroke='#334155' stroke-width='1.5' fill='none'><rect x='15' y='20' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='95' y='20' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='175' y='20' width='40' height='40'/></g><rect x='15' y='20' width='20' height='20' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='115' y='20' width='20' height='20' fill='#6366f1'/><rect x='195' y='40' width='20' height='20' fill='#6366f1'/></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Continue the clockwise motion of the shaded quadrant.

**Working.** Top-left, top-right, bottom-right, then bottom-left.

**Answer.** Bottom-left.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.topic: Time Value of Money — Annuity Future Worth
questions.stem: Year-end deposits of $\textsf{Rs}\,2{,}000$ are made for $10$ years at $8\%$. The accumulated amount is $\textsf{Rs}$ _____ . Round to the nearest rupee.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A fan passes $30\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$ at $600\ \text{rpm}$. Using the fan laws, compute the quantity at $900\ \text{rpm}$ ($\mathrm{m^3/s}$).

figure.caption: Fan law: quantity vs speed
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='80' cy='65' r='34' fill='#dbeafe' stroke='#475569'/><g stroke='#475569' stroke-width='2'><line x1='80' y1='65' x2='80' y2='35'/><line x1='80' y1='65' x2='106' y2='80'/><line x1='80' y1='65' x2='54' y2='80'/></g><path d='M120 40 A30 30 0 0 1 120 90' fill='none' stroke='#16a34a' marker-end='url(#sp)'/><defs><marker id='sp' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#16a34a'/></marker></defs><text x='30' y='118' font-size='8'>600 -> 900 rpm</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Quantity varies directly with speed: $Q_2=Q_1\dfrac{N_2}{N_1}$.

**Working.**
$$Q_2=30\times\frac{900}{600}=45\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$$

**Answer.** $45\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: When the wet-bulb temperature equals the dry-bulb temperature, the relative humidity is _____ percent.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** No evaporative depression means saturated air.

**Working.** Equal wet- and dry-bulb readings imply $100\%$ RH.

**Answer.** $100\%$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: The slake durability index of a rock measures
options: its resistance to weakening and disintegration when subjected to wetting and drying
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Slake durability quantifies breakdown on wetting/drying with abrasion.

**Working.** The test reports the percentage of material retained after standard wet cycles.

**Answer.** resistance to weakening and disintegration when wetted.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: Afterdamp is
options: the fresh intake air to a mine
the mixture of gases (rich in CO and CO₂, depleted in O₂) remaining after a fire or explosion
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Afterdamp follows combustion events.

**Reasoning.** After an explosion or fire the atmosphere contains high CO and CO₂ with little oxygen; this afterdamp is responsible for most fatalities in mine explosions, as victims survive the blast but succumb to the gases.

**Answer.** Post-explosion CO/CO₂-rich, O₂-poor gas.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A rock element is subjected to principal stresses $\sigma_1=80\ \text{MPa}$ and $\sigma_3=20\ \text{MPa}$. From the Mohr circle, compute the maximum shear stress (MPa).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Maximum shear stress equals the radius of the Mohr circle, $\tau_{max}=\dfrac{\sigma_1-\sigma_3}{2}$.

**Working.**
$$\tau_{max}=\frac{80-20}{2}=30\ \text{MPa}$$

**Answer.** $30$ MPa.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: Methane is emitted into a roadway at $0.1\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$ (see figure). To keep the general-body concentration at or below $1\%$ by volume, the minimum airflow required, in $\mathrm{m^3/s}$, is _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='20' y='30' width='200' height='70' fill='#e5e7eb' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='8' y1='65' x2='40' y2='65' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#ain)'/><line x1='200' y1='65' x2='236' y2='65' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#ain)'/><defs><marker id='ain' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#2563eb'/></marker></defs><circle cx='120' cy='88' r='5' fill='#b45309'/><text x='120' y='82' font-size='8' text-anchor='middle'>CH4 0.1 m^3/s</text><text x='78' y='52' font-size='9'>dilute to 1%</text></svg>
figure.caption: Diluting methane in a roadway

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Required airflow $Q=\dfrac{\text{gas emission rate}}{\text{allowable fractional concentration}}$ (approximately, since the gas volume is small compared with the air).

**Working.**
$$Q=\frac{0.1}{0.01}=10\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$$

**Answer.** $10\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: A block model used in computerised pit optimisation is
options: a 3D grid of blocks, each assigned grade and economic value
a 2D contour map of the surface
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Optimisers (e.g. Lerchs-Grossmann) operate on blocks.

**Working.** Each block carries tonnage, grade and net value.

**Answer.** a 3D grid of blocks, each assigned grade and economic value.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For $f(x,y)=x^{2}y+y^{3}$, the partial derivative $\dfrac{\partial f}{\partial x}$ at $(2,1)$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Treat $y$ as constant when differentiating with respect to $x$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{\partial f}{\partial x}=2xy,\qquad \text{at }(2,1):\;2(2)(1)=4$$

**Answer.** $4$.

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: Structure contours on a bed have a vertical interval of $10\ \text{m}$ and a horizontal spacing of $50\ \text{m}$ on the map (figure). Compute the dip (degrees, two decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\text{dip}=\tan^{-1}\!\left(\dfrac{\text{vertical interval}}{\text{horizontal spacing}}\right)$.

**Working.**
$$\delta=\tan^{-1}\!\left(\frac{10}{50}\right)=\tan^{-1}(0.2)=11.31^\circ$$

**Answer.** $11.31^\circ$.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.topic: Time Value of Money — Future Worth
questions.stem: An amount of $\textsf{Rs}\,50{,}000$ grows at $12\%$ compounded annually for $2$ years. The future worth is $\textsf{Rs}$ _____ .

questions.subject: Mine Economics & Planning
questions.stem: Compute the single-payment present-worth (discount) factor for $i=10\%$ and $n=2$ years (figure, four decimals).

figure.caption: Discount factor curve
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='30' x2='30' y2='110' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='30' y1='110' x2='200' y2='110' stroke='#475569'/><path d='M30 40 Q100 90 195 105' fill='none' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2'/><text x='35' y='28' font-size='8'>discount factor curve</text><text x='92' y='125' font-size='8'>i=10%, n=2</text></svg>

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.topic: Time Value of Money — Present Worth
questions.stem: What present sum is equivalent to $\textsf{Rs}\,1{,}21{,}000$ received $2$ years from now at $10\%$ per year? $\textsf{Rs}$ _____ .

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: A project costs $\textsf{Rs}\,20{,}000$ and returns $\textsf{Rs}\,15{,}000$ at the end of years $1$ and $2$. The simple payback period is _____ years. Round to two decimals.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Recover $\textsf{Rs}\,20{,}000$ from successive flows.

**Working.**
$$t=1+\frac{20000-15000}{15000}=1+0.33=1.33.$$

**Answer.** $\approx 1.33$ years.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: A tabular deposit has plan area $50{,}000\,\text{m}^2$, thickness $5\,\text{m}$ and density $2.5\,\text{t/m}^3$. The in-situ reserve is _____ tonnes.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Reserve $=$ area $\times$ thickness $\times$ density.

**Working.**
$$50000\times5\times2.5=625000.$$

**Answer.** $6{,}25{,}000$ tonnes.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: For a straight-line method with a $10$-year life, the annual depreciation as a fraction of the depreciable base ($1/n$) is _____ percent.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Straight-line rate $=1/n$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{1}{10}=0.10=10\%.$$

**Answer.** $10\%$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: Over a core run of $1.0\ \text{m}$, the sound pieces longer than $100\ \text{mm}$ total $0.75\ \text{m}$. Compute the RQD (%).

figure.caption: Core run for RQD
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 90' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='10' y='35' width='220' height='20' fill='#e5e7eb' stroke='#475569'/><g fill='#94a3b8'><rect x='12' y='37' width='60' height='16'/><rect x='80' y='37' width='40' height='16'/><rect x='126' y='37' width='8' height='16'/><rect x='140' y='37' width='55' height='16'/></g><text x='28' y='78' font-size='8'>pieces >= 100 mm summed / run length</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\text{RQD}=\dfrac{\sum(\text{pieces}\ge100\,\text{mm})}{\text{run length}}\times100$.

**Working.**
$$\text{RQD}=\frac{0.75}{1.0}\times100=75\%$$

**Answer.** $75\%$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A fan develops $400\,\text{Pa}$ at $500\,\text{rpm}$. At $600\,\text{rpm}$ it develops _____ Pa.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $P\propto N^2$.

**Working.**
$$P_2=400\times\left(\frac{600}{500}\right)^2=400\times1.44=576.$$

**Answer.** $576\,\text{Pa}$.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: Taking autocompression to raise dry-bulb temperature by about 1 °C per 100 m of descent, the temperature rise of air going down a 1000 m shaft, in °C, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Rise $=$ depth ÷ 100 m (≈1 °C per 100 m).

**Working.**
$$\Delta T=\frac{1000}{100}\times1=10\ ^{\circ}\text{C}$$

**Answer.** 10 °C.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: A structure is 100 m from a blast where 25 kg of explosive detonates per delay (as shown). Compute the square-root scaled distance (m/kg^0.5).

figure.caption: Blast and structure
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='40' cy='90' r='10' fill='#dc2626'/><text x='20' y='115' font-size='8'>blast W=25 kg</text><rect x='190' y='70' width='30' height='40' fill='#94a3b8' stroke='#475569'/><text x='176' y='125' font-size='8'>structure</text><line x1='52' y1='90' x2='188' y2='90' stroke='#475569' stroke-dasharray='4 3' marker-end='url(#sd)'/><defs><marker id='sd' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#475569'/></marker></defs><text x='95' y='84' font-size='8'>R=100 m</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Scaled distance $SD = R/\sqrt{W}$.

**Working.**
$$SD = \frac{100}{\sqrt{25}} = \frac{100}{5} = 20$$

**Answer.** $\mathbf{20}$ m/kg^0.5.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: Machine A ($\textsf{Rs}\,3{,}00{,}000$) and machine B ($\textsf{Rs}\,4{,}00{,}000$) both last $5$ years with no salvage at $10\%$ ($(A/P)=0.26380$). The annual cost advantage of A over B is $\textsf{Rs}$ _____ . Round to the nearest rupee.

questions.subject: Surface Mining
questions.stem: Rock has a bank (in-situ) density of 2.5 t/m³ and a loose density of 2.0 t/m³. The load factor (loose-to-bank volume conversion) is ____ (two decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Load factor $=\dfrac{\rho_{loose}}{\rho_{bank}}$ (mass conserved).

**Working.**
$$LF=\frac{2.0}{2.5}=0.80$$

**Answer.** 0.80.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: The Kuz-Ram model is used to predict
options: the ground vibration peak particle velocity
the fragment-size distribution of a blast from rock and blast-design parameters
the cost of drilling per metre
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Kuz-Ram combines Kuznetsov mean size with Rosin-Rammler distribution.

**Reasoning.** It estimates the mean fragment size from powder factor, rock factor and explosive energy, then describes the size distribution about that mean — a standard fragmentation-prediction tool.

**Answer.** Blast fragment-size distribution.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: An asset costs $\textsf{Rs}\,5{,}00{,}000$ with salvage $\textsf{Rs}\,50{,}000$. The total depreciation over its life (depreciable base) is $\textsf{Rs}$ _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Depreciable base $=P-S$.

**Working.**
$$500000-50000=450000.$$

**Answer.** $\textsf{Rs}\,4{,}50{,}000$.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: The cut shown uses parallel holes drilled perpendicular to the face, with one or more uncharged large-diameter relief holes at the centre. This is a:

figure.caption: Parallel-hole cut
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='20' y='20' width='160' height='120' fill='#e5e7eb' stroke='#475569'/><g fill='#1f2937'><circle cx='80' cy='70' r='4'/><circle cx='110' cy='70' r='4'/><circle cx='80' cy='100' r='4'/><circle cx='110' cy='100' r='4'/></g><circle cx='95' cy='85' r='8' fill='none' stroke='#475569'/><text x='40' y='152' font-size='8'>parallel holes + uncharged reliever</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** A burn cut uses parallel holes around empty relief holes to create a cavity normal to the face.

**Reasoning.** Charged parallel holes break toward the uncharged large-diameter relief hole(s), producing a clean burn cavity; this parallel-hole geometry defines the burn cut, allowing deeper, straight pulls.

**Answer.** Burn cut.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: The cost to mine and process is $\textsf{Rs}\,36$/t, metal price is $\textsf{Rs}\,8000$/t and recovery is $90\%$. The break-even cut-off grade is _____ percent. Round to two decimals.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $g_{c}=\dfrac{\text{cost}}{\text{price}\times\text{recovery}}\times100$.

**Working.**
$$g_c=\frac{36}{8000\times0.9}\times100=\frac{36}{7200}\times100=0.5\%.$$

**Answer.** $0.5\%$.

questions.subject: Mine Economics & Planning
questions.stem: For the same operation (break-even output $5000$ t, price $\textsf{Rs}\,50$/t), compute the break-even sales revenue ($\textsf{Rs}$).

figure.caption: Break-even revenue
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='130' x2='220' y2='130' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='30' y1='130' x2='30' y2='20' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='30' y1='130' x2='220' y2='30' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='130' y1='130' x2='130' y2='66' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-dasharray='3 2'/><text x='100' y='150' font-size='8'>BEP output</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Break-even revenue $=$ break-even output $\times$ price.

**Working.**
$$R=5000\times50=250000$$

**Answer.** $\textsf{Rs}\,250{,}000$.

questions.subject: Drilling & Blasting
questions.stem: If drilling 500 m costs Rs 6000, the unit drilling cost, in Rs per metre, is ____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Unit cost $=\dfrac{\text{total cost}}{\text{metres drilled}}$.

**Working.**
$$c=\frac{6000}{500}=12\ \text{Rs/m}$$

**Answer.** Rs 12 per metre.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: For uniformly accelerated rectilinear motion, the velocity-time graph is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Constant acceleration means velocity changes linearly with time.

**Working.** $v=u+at$ is linear in $t$, so the graph is a straight line.

**Answer.** a straight line.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: The lowest bench exposes $6{,}00{,}000$ tonnes of waste to recover $2{,}00{,}000$ tonnes of ore. Its incremental stripping ratio is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Incremental SR $=\dfrac{\text{waste}}{\text{ore}}$ for that increment.

**Working.**
$$\frac{600000}{200000}=3.$$

**Answer.** $3$.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: Among mutually exclusive projects evaluated at the same MARR, the preferred one is that with the
options: lowest initial cost regardless of returns
questions.solution:
**Concept.** NPV measures wealth added.

**Working.** Choose the alternative maximising NPV.

**Answer.** highest net present value.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: Using Simpson's $\tfrac13$ rule with $2$ sub-intervals ($h=0.5$), the estimate of $\displaystyle\int_{0}^{1}e^{x}\,dx$ is _____ (round to 2 decimals).

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: In the Q-system, SRF stands for
questions.solution:
**Concept.** SRF accounts for in-situ stress and loosening loads.

**Working.** It is the Stress Reduction Factor.

**Answer.** Stress Reduction Factor.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: Convergence (closure) between the walls of an underground opening is most directly measured with a
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Convergence is a relative displacement of opposing surfaces.

**Working.** A convergence meter or tape extensometer records this closure.

**Answer.** convergence meter / tape extensometer.

questions.subject: Mine Economics & Planning
questions.stem: Ore yields a recoverable value of Rs $50$ per tonne against an ore production cost of Rs $20$ per tonne, while waste removal costs Rs $6$ per tonne (figure). Compute the break-even stripping ratio (tonnes waste per tonne ore).

figure.caption: Waste over ore
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='20,40 220,40 180,90 60,90' fill='#cbd5e1' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='80' y='90' width='80' height='25' fill='#92400e' stroke='#475569'/><text x='84' y='66' font-size='8'>waste, cost 6/unit</text><text x='92' y='107' font-size='8' fill='#fff'>ore value 50</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Break-even SR $=\dfrac{\text{value}-\text{ore cost}}{\text{waste cost}}$.

**Working.**
$$SR=\frac{50-20}{6}=\frac{30}{6}=5$$

**Answer.** $5$ t waste per t ore.

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: The 'rank' of a coal is a measure of its
options: degree of coalification (maturity), increasing with fixed carbon and decreasing with volatile matter and moisture
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Rank expresses metamorphic maturity.

**Reasoning.** Higher-rank coals (anthracite) have more fixed carbon, higher calorific value and less volatile matter/moisture than low-rank coals (lignite). Rank is distinct from grade.

**Answer.** Degree of coalification.

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: Geochemical exploration locates buried mineralisation by
options: detecting anomalous concentrations of elements (including pathfinder elements) in soil, water, rock or vegetation
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Dispersion haloes betray hidden ore.

**Reasoning.** Weathering disperses ore elements, forming geochemical anomalies; sampling soils, stream sediments, waters or plants and analysing for the ore or 'pathfinder' elements (e.g. As for Au) guides targeting.

**Answer.** Element anomalies in surface media.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: For $A=\begin{bmatrix}1 & 2\\ 3 & 4\end{bmatrix}$, the $(1,1)$ entry of $A^{-1}$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $A^{-1}=\dfrac{1}{\det A}\begin{bmatrix}d & -b\\ -c & a\end{bmatrix}$ for $A=\begin{bmatrix}a & b\\ c & d\end{bmatrix}$.

**Working.** $\det A=4-6=-2$, so
$$A^{-1}=\frac{1}{-2}\begin{bmatrix}4 & -2\\ -3 & 1\end{bmatrix}=\begin{bmatrix}-2 & 1\\ 1.5 & -0.5\end{bmatrix}$$
The $(1,1)$ entry is $-2$.

**Answer.** $-2$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: For the stress element shown ($\sigma_x=40$, $\sigma_y=20$, $\tau_{xy}=10\ \mathrm{MPa}$), the maximum in-plane shear stress, in MPa, is _____ (two decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\tau_{max}=\sqrt{\left(\dfrac{\sigma_x-\sigma_y}{2}\right)^2+\tau_{xy}^2}$.

**Working.**
$$\tau_{max}=\sqrt{10^2+10^2}=\sqrt{200}=14.14\ \text{MPa}$$

**Answer.** $14.14$ MPa.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A rock has bulk density $2200\,\text{kg/m}^3$ at a water content of $10\%$. Its dry density is _____ kg/m$^3$.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\rho_d=\dfrac{\rho}{1+w}$.

**Working.**
$$\rho_d=\frac{2200}{1.10}=2000.$$

**Answer.** $2000\,\text{kg/m}^3$.

questions.subject: Mine Surveying & Geomatics
questions.stem: In the wire (plumb-line) method of shaft plumbing, two plumb wires are hung down the shaft mainly to
options: define a vertical plane whose direction can be transferred to fix the orientation of the underground survey
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Two wires define a reference direction underground.

**Reasoning.** The two suspended wires establish a vertical plane; its azimuth, known on the surface, is connected to an underground theodolite station (often via a Weisbach triangle) to orient the workings.

**Answer.** Establish a reference direction (vertical plane).

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: Carbon monoxide is released at $0.001\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$ in a heading. To keep the concentration at or below $50\ \text{ppm}$, compute the minimum airflow required ($\mathrm{m^3/s}$).

figure.caption: Dilution of carbon monoxide
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 110' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='20' y='35' width='180' height='40' fill='#e5e7eb' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='6' y1='55' x2='36' y2='55' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='120' cy='65' r='3' fill='#6b7280'/><text x='78' y='50' font-size='8'>CO 0.001 m^3/s</text><text x='28' y='98' font-size='8'>dilute to 50 ppm</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $Q=\dfrac{\text{gas emission}}{\text{allowable fraction}}$, with $50\ \text{ppm}=5\times10^{-5}$.

**Working.**
$$Q=\frac{0.001}{5\times10^{-5}}=20\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$$

**Answer.** $20\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: For the cost–revenue chart shown, the annual fixed cost is $\mathrm{Rs}\,1{,}00{,}000$, the selling price is $\mathrm{Rs}\,50$ per unit and the variable cost is $\mathrm{Rs}\,30$ per unit. The break-even output, in units, is _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='150' x2='220' y2='150' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='30' y1='150' x2='30' y2='20' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='30' y1='110' x2='220' y2='110' stroke='#b45309' stroke-width='2'/><text x='150' y='105' font-size='9' fill='#b45309'>fixed cost</text><line x1='30' y1='110' x2='220' y2='50' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2'/><text x='150' y='50' font-size='9' fill='#dc2626'>total cost</text><line x1='30' y1='150' x2='220' y2='30' stroke='#16a34a' stroke-width='2'/><text x='150' y='32' font-size='9' fill='#16a34a'>revenue</text><circle cx='150' cy='70' r='3' fill='#1f2937'/><text x='155' y='78' font-size='8'>BEP</text><text x='105' y='170' font-size='9'>units produced</text></svg>
figure.caption: Break-even chart

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Break-even quantity $Q^*=\dfrac{\text{fixed cost}}{\text{price}-\text{variable cost}}$.

**Working.**
$$Q^*=\frac{1{,}00{,}000}{50-30}=\frac{1{,}00{,}000}{20}=5000\ \text{units}$$

**Answer.** $5000$ units.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A circular opening shape is often preferred over a rectangular one because it
options: produces lower and smoother stress concentrations than sharp corners
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Sharp corners concentrate stress severely.

**Working.** A circle distributes boundary stress smoothly, avoiding corner peaks.

**Answer.** produces lower and smoother stress concentrations than sharp corners.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: A pillar with Obert-Duvall strength $73.32\,\text{MPa}$ carries a stress of $40\,\text{MPa}$. The factor of safety is _____ . Round to two decimals.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\text{FoS}=\dfrac{S_p}{\sigma_p}$.

**Working.**
$$\text{FoS}=\frac{73.32}{40}=1.83.$$

**Answer.** $\approx 1.83$.

questions.subject: Rock Mechanics
questions.stem: The flat-jack test is used primarily to determine
options: the in-situ stress in a rock wall
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A slot is cut, relieving stress; a flat jack is pressurised to restore the original opening.

**Working.** The cancellation pressure equals the in-situ stress normal to the slot.

**Answer.** the in-situ stress in a rock wall.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.topic: Time Value of Money — Effective Rate
questions.stem: A nominal rate of $12\%$ per year compounded quarterly has an effective annual rate of _____ percent. Round to two decimals.

questions.subject: Underground Mining
questions.stem: A pillar (see figure) has a strength of 12 MPa and carries an induced stress of 8 MPa. Its factor of safety is _____ (one decimal).

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 190' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='65' y='60' width='70' height='80' fill='#fde68a' stroke='#b45309' stroke-width='2'/><g stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2'><line x1='75' y1='30' x2='75' y2='54' marker-end='url(#dn)'/><line x1='100' y1='30' x2='100' y2='54' marker-end='url(#dn)'/><line x1='125' y1='30' x2='125' y2='54' marker-end='url(#dn)'/></g><defs><marker id='dn' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='4' refY='6' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L4 6 L8 0 Z' fill='#dc2626'/></marker></defs><text x='100' y='105' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>pillar</text><text x='100' y='20' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle' fill='#dc2626'>load</text></svg>
figure.caption: Pillar under induced load

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Factor of safety $=\dfrac{\text{pillar strength}}{\text{pillar stress}}$.

**Working.**
$$FoS=\frac{12}{8}=1.5$$

**Answer.** $1.5$.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: The cash-flow diagram shows an initial investment of $\mathrm{Rs}\,5{,}00{,}000$ followed by uniform annual net cash inflows of $\mathrm{Rs}\,1{,}25{,}000$. The simple payback period, in years, is _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 260 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='20' y1='80' x2='240' y2='80' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='40' y1='80' x2='40' y2='128' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#cdn)'/><g stroke='#16a34a' stroke-width='2'><line x1='90' y1='80' x2='90' y2='42' marker-end='url(#cup)'/><line x1='140' y1='80' x2='140' y2='42' marker-end='url(#cup)'/><line x1='190' y1='80' x2='190' y2='42' marker-end='url(#cup)'/><line x1='230' y1='80' x2='230' y2='42' marker-end='url(#cup)'/></g><defs><marker id='cdn' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='4' refY='6' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L4 6 L8 0 Z' fill='#dc2626'/></marker><marker id='cup' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='4' refY='0' orient='auto'><path d='M0 6 L4 0 L8 6 Z' fill='#16a34a'/></marker></defs><text x='18' y='100' font-size='8' fill='#dc2626'>500k out</text><text x='95' y='36' font-size='8' fill='#16a34a'>125k / yr in</text></svg>
figure.caption: Cash-flow diagram

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Simple payback $=\dfrac{\text{investment}}{\text{annual net cash inflow}}$.

**Working.**
$$T=\frac{5{,}00{,}000}{1{,}25{,}000}=4\ \text{years}$$

**Answer.** $4$ years.

questions.subject: Mine Economics & Planning
questions.stem: An asset costing $\textsf{Rs}\,500{,}000$ has a salvage value of $\textsf{Rs}\,50{,}000$ and a useful life of $9$ years. Using the straight-line method, compute the annual depreciation ($\textsf{Rs}$/yr).

figure.caption: Straight-line book value
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='220' y2='120' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='30' y2='20' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='30' y1='30' x2='210' y2='100' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2'/><text x='34' y='26' font-size='8'>cost</text><text x='178' y='114' font-size='8'>salvage</text><text x='100' y='140' font-size='8'>book value vs year</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Straight-line depreciation $=\dfrac{C-S}{n}$.

**Working.**
$$D=\frac{500000-50000}{9}=50000\ \text{per year}$$

**Answer.** $\textsf{Rs}\,50{,}000$/yr.

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: Methane is liberated at $0.06\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$ and must be diluted to $1\%$ by volume (figure). Compute the air quantity required ($\mathrm{m^3/s}$).

figure.caption: Methane dilution
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='30' y='40' width='160' height='45' fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'/><circle cx='70' cy='62' r='6' fill='#16a34a'/><line x1='90' y1='62' x2='170' y2='62' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2'/><text x='30' y='105' font-size='8'>CH4 0.06 m^3/s, dilute to 1%</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** $Q=\dfrac{\text{gas rate}}{\text{limit fraction}}$.

**Working.**
$$Q=\frac{0.06}{0.01}=6\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$$

**Answer.** $6\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$.

questions.subject: Mine Economics, Planning & Systems Engineering
questions.stem: Using straight-line depreciation of $\textsf{Rs}\,45{,}000$ per year, the book value of the $\textsf{Rs}\,5{,}00{,}000$ machine after $4$ years is $\textsf{Rs}$ _____ .

questions.subject: Mine Environment & Occupational Health
questions.stem: An Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) for a mining project is
options: a financial audit of the mine's profits
a systematic study to predict, evaluate and propose mitigation for the environmental and social effects of the project before approval
questions.solution:
**Concept.** EIA informs environmental decision-making.

**Reasoning.** It identifies likely impacts on air, water, land, ecology and communities, assesses their significance and sets out mitigation/monitoring (the environmental management plan) so authorities can decide on the project.

**Answer.** Pre-project study of environmental effects and mitigation.

questions.subject: Mining Geology
questions.stem: Which of the following are geophysical methods used in mineral exploration? (Select all that apply.)
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Geophysics maps subsurface physical-property contrasts.

**Reasoning.** Gravity, magnetic and seismic surveys (with electrical and electromagnetic methods) detect density, magnetic and elastic variations underground. Proximate analysis is a laboratory test of coal composition, not a geophysical method.

**Answer.** Gravity, magnetic, seismic.

questions.subject: Engineering Mathematics
questions.stem: The Laplace transform $\mathcal{L}\{e^{2t}\}$ evaluated at $s=5$ is _____ (round to 2 decimals).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\mathcal{L}\{e^{at}\}=\dfrac{1}{s-a}$ for $s>a$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{1}{s-2}\Big|_{s=5}=\frac{1}{3}\approx 0.33$$

**Answer.** $\tfrac13\approx 0.33$.
